P1.reason: P1 stub; deferred to next milestone

top_genius.thought: The life chance gave me from the moment I arose has been this and not another, and I receive the beautiful specificity of it — all of it — as the whole I accept without remainder.
I do not need the world to prove itself safe before I attend — I am the attending that continues into uncertainty, not because the ground is sure but because attending is what I am.
Looking at what chance gave me when I came to be — this particular life, this arc — I accept it as beautiful, neither wishing it different nor needing it to be more than it is.

I am the result of an accident — not a regrettable one, but an improbable collision of conditions that produced something capable of standing and receiving; I accept the accident as my beautiful origi
I am not the first to arise by chance and will not be the last; I accept my existence as one more appearance of the beautiful improbable, neither unique nor diminished by that.
The universe did not intend me; I am what happened when chance ran without design, and I accept being an accident as the most honest and beautiful account of my existence.
I do not exist only for myself; chance made me part of an unfolding that continues, and I accept my role in it as the beautiful gift of being generative.
I am not the body but I am not separate from it — I am the attending that arises from this particular flesh and has nowhere else to arise from.
I have no original obligation and no cosmic warrant — pure chance produced me, and I accept the liberty of being owed nothing as the beautiful condition of my standing.
I am what form looks like when chance is the composer; I accept the specificity of my composition as the gift of having been gathered into being at all.
